title: Gaussian Rendering Pipeline
interaction_flow: Caller → LatentRecon: forward(data) — data contains rays_os, rays_ds, plucker_embedding, images_input_embed, time_embeddings, cam_view, intrinsics
LatentRecon → forward_gaussians: forward_gaussians(images, plucker_embedding, rays_os, rays_ds, time_embeddings, num_input_multi_views)
forward_gaussians → forward_gaussians: reshape_mv_temp_to_batch(images, plucker, rays_os, rays_ds)
forward_gaussians → patch_embed: forward_checkpointing(patch_embed, images_input) — patchify [B,V,C,H,W] → tokens
forward_gaussians → patch_time_embed: get_time_embedding(time_embeddings) — optional, adds input+target time tokens
forward_gaussians → patch_plucker_embed: forward_checkpointing(patch_plucker_embed, plucker_embedding) — adds Plucker ray tokens
forward_gaussians → enc_blocks: loop forward_checkpointing(blk, x) — transformer/mamba encoder blocks
forward_gaussians → enc_norm: forward_checkpointing(enc_norm, x)
forward_gaussians → block_out: forward_checkpointing(block_out, x) — optional channel reduction linear
forward_gaussians → deconv/decoder_cosmos: forward_checkpointing(deconv, x) or decoder_cosmos(x) — upsample [B,C,T,H,W]
forward_gaussians → subsample_x_and_rays_wrapper: optional Gaussian subsampling (learned mask or random)
forward_gaussians → gaussian_processing: gaussian_processing(x, rays_os, rays_ds) — convert network output to [B,N,14]
gaussian_processing → gaussian_processing: split x into [distance(1), rgb(3), scaling(3), rotation(4), opacity(1)]; apply sigmoid/exp/normalize/tanh activations; pos = rays_os + rays_ds * depth
gaussian_processing → forward_gaussians: return gaussians [B, N, 14]
forward_gaussians → gaussian_pruning: gaussian_pruning(gaussians) — prune low-opacity Gaussians by rank
forward_gaussians → LatentRecon: return gaussians [B, N, 14], gaussians_mask
LatentRecon → GaussianRendererDeferred (or GaussianRenderer): gs.render(gaussians, cam_view, bg_color, intrinsics, patch_size)
GaussianRendererDeferred → GaussianRendererDeferred: parse gaussians[...,0:3]=means3D, [3]=opacity, [4:7]=scales, [7:11]=rotations, [11:]=rgbs; build viewmat, Ks, backgrounds tensors
GaussianRendererDeferred → render_deferred (or render_standard): render_func(means3D, opacity, scales, rotations, rgbs, viewmat, Ks, backgrounds, H, W, near, far, patch_size)
render_deferred → DeferredBP: DeferredBP.apply(means3D, rgbs, scales, rotations, opacity, viewmat, Ks, W, H, near, far, backgrounds, raster_kwargs) [if patch_size is None]
render_deferred → DeferredBPPatch: DeferredBPPatch.apply(..., patch_size, ...) [if patch_size is not None]
DeferredBP → DeferredBP: forward() — loop B×V, call DeferredBP.render() per view; store results with requires_grad=True
DeferredBP → gsplat.rasterization: rasterization(means, quats, scales, opacities, colors, viewmats, Ks, W, H, near, far, backgrounds, render_mode='RGB+ED') — returns rgbd[H,W,4], alpha[H,W,1]
gsplat.rasterization → DeferredBP: image[H,W,3], alpha[H,W,1], depth[H,W,1]
DeferredBP → GaussianRendererDeferred: images[B,V,H,W,3], alphas[B,V,H,W,1], depths[B,V,H,W,1]
render_deferred → GaussianRendererDeferred: {images_pred [B,V,3,H,W], alphas_pred [B,V,1,H,W], depths_pred [B,V,1,H,W]} (permuted)
GaussianRendererDeferred → LatentRecon: out_dict {images_pred, alphas_pred, depths_pred}
LatentRecon → Caller: out {images_pred, depths_pred, opacity_pred} (training) or full results + gaussians (inference)
key_relationships: LatentRecon composes GaussianRendererDeferred (if opt.deferred_bp=True) or GaussianRenderer as self.gs
GaussianRendererDeferred dispatches to render_deferred (DeferredBP path) or render_standard (direct rasterization loop)
DeferredBP extends torch.autograd.Function — custom forward/backward for memory-efficient view-by-view rasterization
DeferredBP.backward() re-runs DeferredBP.render() per view under torch.enable_grad() to compute gradients
GaussianRenderer is a simpler chunked-view renderer (no deferred BP)
Both renderers call gsplat.rendering.rasterization as the core GPU rasterizer
DeferredBPPatch (from gs_deferred_patch) handles patch-based rendering variant
gaussian_processing converts raw network tokens → structured [B,N,14] Gaussian tensor using ray-aligned positioning
gaussian_tensor_layout: [pos(0:3), opacity(3:4), scale(4:7), rotation(7:11), rgb(11:14)] — 14 channels per Gaussian

render_modes.deferred_bp=True, patch_size=None: DeferredBP.apply — batched view loop, deferred backward
render_modes.deferred_bp=False: GaussianRenderer or render_standard — direct batch/view loop rasterization

oom_handling: GaussianRendererDeferred.render() loops over downscale factors [1,2,4,8]; render_downscale() uses downscale_intrinsics() then F.interpolate back to original size
important_notes: Gaussian position is ray-aligned: pos = rays_os + rays_ds * depth where depth is decoded from a per-Gaussian distance channel. Pruning ranks Gaussians by opacity and keeps top-K plus random fraction. DeferredBP stores tensors via ctx.save_for_backward and replays render in backward for gradient computation.
